strengths: Genuine distributed-systems depth: production low-latency microservices at Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second, directly relevant to real-time inference and event-streaming requirements.

Meaningful fintech exposure spanning investment infrastructure (RIDE Capital) and pricing/working-capital optimization (Optilyz), providing domain familiarity the role treats as a meaningful advantage.

Early but hands-on agentic AI involvement at re:cinq, including MCP framework development and agentic workflow tooling, signaling active engagement with the exact technical domain the role centers on.
concerns: No executive or engineering-management experience on record: the resume shows no people management, no team scaling, and no organizational leadership — the role requires a track record of growing teams from 10 to 30+ with a senior leadership layer underneath.

Agentic AI work is tooling and infrastructure support, not ownership of production multi-agent systems with financial or operational consequences; the role explicitly excludes prototype or research contexts.
No evidence of compliance, auditability, or regulated-environment operating experience — a hard requirement given multi-jurisdictional fintech infrastructure and credit-risk systems.
response.justification: Bogdan brings solid distributed-systems engineering and relevant fintech domain exposure, and his current work on agentic tooling at re:cinq shows he is moving in the right technical direction. However, the role is an executive position requiring demonstrated organizational leadership, team scaling, and production ownership of multi-agent AI in regulated environments — none of which appear in his history. The gap is not a matter of depth in one area but of the entire leadership and operating profile the CTO role demands.
